thinking:
**Analyzing Candidate Contacts**

I'm currently focused on the candidate's contact information, specifically within lines 3690-3701 of the code. I'm exploring the structure and how it's presented, looking for opportunities to refine the design for optimal clarity and user experience. My attention is on the flexbox layout.


**Integrating WhatsApp Button**

I've been working on integrating a WhatsApp button into the candidate card. The user's request is for a quick WhatsApp access in the first column. I've formulated a plan to create a direct WhatsApp link using the candidate's clean phone number, and it seems I'll need to parse the phone data and remove any non-digit characters. I've also come up with the logic to handle different phone number formats, including adding the '55' country code if needed, and generating the final WhatsApp link. I'm considering design alternatives, either a standalone button or integration with the phone entry.


**Designing WhatsApp Button**

I'm now focused on the button's visual design. I am weighing options, like using a `MessageSquare` icon from Lucide-react styled in green, importing a custom SVG WhatsApp icon for a premium look, or even crafting a "WhatsApp" button. I'm leaning toward the custom SVG for its sleekness and wondering about its size and color.


**Refining Visual Integration**

I've decided to refine the visual integration of the WhatsApp icon. The goal is to seamlessly incorporate it with the phone entry. Now I'm focused on a perfect visual integration, exploring different placement options alongside the phone number. I'm aiming for a subtle yet clear signal to click and open a WhatsApp chat, with a focus on an impeccable look and feel.


**Implementing WhatsApp Button**

I've got the WhatsApp button fully implemented now. I've focused on styling it for visual appeal, using the 'Chamar no WhatsApp' title. The button is a link that opens a WhatsApp chat directly, with a custom SVG icon and green theme.


**Positioning WhatsApp Button**

I'm now integrating the WhatsApp button within `CompanyDashboard.tsx`, specifically within the candidate contact block. I've decided the best placement is adjacent to the phone number, aligning with user expectations for a quick contact option. I'm focusing on the visual harmony of the button alongside the existing phone information, aiming for a seamless user experience. The button will be styled with an emerald and white color scheme (`bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-700 hover:bg-emerald-100 border-emerald-200/50`) for a professional look.


**Refactoring Contact Display**

I'm now focusing on directly modifying the candidate contact display within the code. I'll use the `replace_file_content` tool on lines 3690-3701 to incorporate my changes, streamlining the process. The immediate goal is to update the contact details integration with the WhatsApp button, ensuring a smooth transition for users.

args.Description: "Adiciona o botão de WhatsApp verde com link direto wa.me na seção de contatos rápidos do candidato"
args.Instruction: "Substituir a div de contatos do candidato para incluir o botão de WhatsApp ao lado do telefone."
args.ReplacementContent:
" {/* Contatos do Candidato */}
{fullApp.candidate_email} • {parseCandidatePhoneData(fullApp.candidate_phone).phone} •
args.TargetContent:
" {/* Contatos do Candidato */}
{fullApp.candidate_email} • {parseCandidatePhoneData(fullApp.candidate_phone).phone}
"
args.toolAction: "Adicionar botão de WhatsApp verde no card do candidato"
